summary: Monumental Black Peter & jam marathon with tight set
blurb: The show delivers a monster second‑set jam marathon highlighted by a soaring Black Peter and a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition. Jerry’s tone shines throughout, while the drums/space segment is dark and moody. Standout moments include Sugar Magnolia, Playing In The Band > Uncle John’s Band, and an inspired I Need A Miracle.
review:
April 27, 1991 at the Sam Boyd Silver Bowl captured a high‑energy Vegas night that still feels fresh for Deadheads. The first set builds from a quirky Touch of Grey opening into a powerful Might As Well closer, but it’s the second set that truly defines this performance – a nonstop jam marathon anchored by a monster Black Peter and an unforgettable Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue. Jerry Garcia’s tone is sweet on Friend Of The Devil and ferocious on Black Peter, while the drums/space stretch is described as dark, moody and exceptionally tight. Sugar Magnolia opens the second set with classic sparkle, and the sequence Playing In The Band > Uncle John’s Band rolls into a powerful Drums → Space that many reviewers call “the highlight of the weekend.” Keyboard duties are split between Bruce Hornsby’s piano/accordion (polarizing but often praised) and Vince Welnick’s contributions, which range from “actually OK” to “marginal utility.” Overall, despite occasional EQ quirks and a few drop‑outs on Touch, Loose Lucy and The Weight in some recordings, the musical chemistry and improvisational peaks make this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that early‑90s Dead magic.

The recommended source is the Vernon‑Fink soundboard (gd91-04-27.sbd.vernon-fink.12018.sbeok.shnf), which balances clarity with just enough audience ambience to preserve the live feel while delivering most of the iconic moments in high fidelity.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across five of seven recordings: repeated praise for Black Peter, Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, tight drums/space, and Jerry's tone; no consistent negative comments about performance quality; mixed remarks focus only on recording quirks.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with only minor EQ issues; preserves the energy of the monster second set and highlights the standout songs.

key_highlights: Black Peter – a soaring, monster solo that defines the night
Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain – seamless segue praised by all recordings
Second‑set jam marathon culminating in dark, moody Drums → Space
Sugar Magnolia and Playing In The Band > Uncle John’s Band provide classic peaks

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is sweet on Friend Of The Devil and monster on Black Peter; early mix occasionally buries his guitar but overall his solos are repeatedly praised.
band_performance.Bruce Hornsby: Hornsby's piano/accordion work divides opinion – some love the added texture, others note missed cues and occasional overcrowding in the mix.
band_performance.Vince Welnick: Welnick is described as ‘actually OK’ by some reviewers but also called of ‘marginal utility’; a few slip‑ups are noted during vocals and piano parts.
band_performance.Mickey: Percussion solid throughout, especially leading into Space; no major complaints.
band_performance.Billy: Drums provide a dark, moody foundation that shines on the Drums → Space segment.
